title: Cloud Computing & Govt Initiatives – Page 496
section: Computer: Cloud Computing, Encryption, Government Digital Initiatives

questions.question: Cloud Computing is on demand delivery of computer power, database storage, applications and other IT resources through:

options.B: A cloud services platform mainly via the internet

questions.explanation: Page 496: Cloud Computing – On demand delivery of resources through a cloud services platform mainly via the internet.

questions.question: Cloud Computing is a distributed computer architecture that provides software, infrastructure and:

options.B: Platforms just as required by applications/users

questions.explanation: Page 496: It is a distributed computer architecture that provides software, infrastructure and platforms just as required by applications/users.

questions.question: Spreadsheet/MS Excel is appropriate for performing:
questions.explanation: Page 496: Spreadsheet/MS Excel is appropriate for performing numerical and statistic calculations.

questions.question: CAD/CAM systems are based on:
questions.explanation: Page 496: CAD/CAM systems are based on Interactive Computer Graphics.

questions.question: Data Encryption Standard (DES) is an implementation of a Feistel Cipher. It uses how many round Feistel structure?
questions.explanation: Page 496: DES uses 16 round Feistel structure and the block size is 64 bits.

questions.question: The block size of DES (Data Encryption Standard) is:
questions.explanation: Page 496: DES uses 16 round Feistel structure and the block size is 64 bits.

questions.question: The Banker algorithm is a resource allocation and deadlock avoidance algorithm developed by:
questions.explanation: Page 496: The Banker algorithm is a resource allocation and deadlock avoidance algorithm developed by Edsger W. Dijkstra.

questions.question: Gateway is used to connect two different networks using:
questions.explanation: Page 496: Gateway is used to connect two different networks using different protocols.

questions.question: A half byte is known as 'Nibble' and consists of:
questions.explanation: Page 496: A half byte is known as 'Nibble' (consists 4 bits).

questions.question: MS Excel from Version 8.0 to 11.0 could handle 65,536 rows and:
questions.explanation: Page 496: MS Excel Version 8.0 to 11.0 could handle 65,536 rows and 256 columns.

questions.question: MS Excel Version 12.0 can handle 10,48,576 rows and:
questions.explanation: Page 496: Later Version 12.0 can handle 10,48,576 rows and 16,384 columns.

questions.question: The robot named 'Pepe' was recently used by pupils at a primary school in:
questions.explanation: Page 496: The robot named Pepe was recently used by pupils at a primary school in Kerala.

questions.question: India's first indigenously developed payment ecosystem for transport is based on:
questions.explanation: Page 496: India's first indigenously developed payment ecosystem for transport, based on National Common Mobility Card (NCMC) standards, launched on 4th March, 2019.

questions.question: Mobile App 'cVIGIL' has been launched by:
questions.explanation: Page 496: Mobile App 'cVIGIL' has been launched by Election Commission of India for citizens to report Model Code of Conduct and Expenditure violations during elections.

questions.question: VISHWAS stands for Video Integration and State Wide Advanced:
questions.explanation: Page 496: VISHWAS is acronym for Video Integration and State Wide Advanced Security.

questions.question: VISHWAS project aims at improving:

options.B: Law and Order Management, Proactive traffic management and control, Crime detection

questions.explanation: Page 496: VISHWAS aims at improving Law and Order Management, Proactive traffic management and control, Crime detection through video analytics.

questions.question: A gutter margin adds extra space to the side margin, top margin, or inside margins of a document that you plan to:
questions.explanation: Page 496: A gutter margin adds extra space to margins of a document you plan to bind.

questions.question: An integrated circuit (IC) is small chip that can function as an amplifier, oscillator, timer, microprocessor, or even computer memory. It is usually made of:
questions.explanation: Page 496: An IC is usually made of silicon, can hold hundreds to millions of transistors, resistors and capacitors.

questions.question: WorldWideWeb: First Web browser was developed in the mid and late 1980s. Tim Berners-Lee is credited with developing it in:
questions.explanation: Page 496: Tim Berners-Lee is credited with developing in 1990, both the first web server and the first web browser called WorldWideWeb (later renamed Nexus).

questions.question: The first web browser was called WorldWideWeb and was later renamed:
questions.explanation: Page 496: The first web browser was called WorldWideWeb (without spaces) and later renamed Nexus.

questions.question: Windows 10 was released as a preview on:
questions.explanation: Page 496: Windows 10 was released as a preview on 1st October, 2014.

questions.question: Windows 11 was released on:
questions.explanation: Page 496: Windows 11 was released on 5th October, 2021.

questions.question: e-Pramaan is a framework for:
questions.explanation: Page 496: e-Pramaan: Framework for e-Authentication for public services. Objective is to deliver government services securely.

questions.question: Online e-Sign (e-Hastakshar) facility was formally launched in:
questions.explanation: Page 496: Online e-Sign (e-Hastakshar) facility was formally launched in 2015.

questions.question: e-Sign service facilitates instant signing of documents online by citizens in:
questions.explanation: Page 496: e-Sign facilitates instant signing of documents online by citizens in a legally acceptable form.

questions.question: National Knowledge Network (NKN) was established in:
questions.explanation: Page 496: National Knowledge Network (NKN) was established in 2010.

questions.question: The objective of NKN is to interconnect all institutions of higher learning and research with:
questions.explanation: Page 496: NKN objective: interconnect all institutions of higher learning and research with a high-speed data communication network.

questions.question: NKN is the only network globally that carries research and evaluation, internet and e-Governance traffic as:

options.B: Three independent verticals under one umbrella

questions.explanation: Page 496: NKN is the only network globally that carries research/evaluation, internet and e-Governance traffic as three independent verticals under one umbrella.

questions.question: UMANG stands for:

options.A: Universal Mobile Access for New Generation
options.B: Unified Mobile Application for New-Age Governance
options.C: United Mobile App for National Government

questions.explanation: Page 496: UMANG: Unified Mobile Application for New-Age Governance.

questions.question: UMANG was developed and launched in:
questions.explanation: Page 496: UMANG has been developed and launched in 2017 as a single mobile platform to deliver major government services.

questions.question: UMANG's core platform is integrated with:

options.B: Adhaar, DigiLocker, PayGov, Rapid Assessment System (RAS) etc.

questions.explanation: Page 496: UMANG's core platform is integrated with Adhaar, DigiLocker, PayGov, Rapid Assessment System (RAS) etc.

questions.question: UMANG supports how many Indian languages?
questions.explanation: Page 496: UMANG supports 12 Indian languages, in addition to English.

questions.question: Over how many services from central/states departments are on boarded into UMANG?
questions.explanation: Page 496: Over 2,033 services from 207 central/states departments are on boarded into UMANG.

questions.question: Digital Locker (Digilocker) is a key initiative under:
questions.explanation: Page 496: Digital Locker (Digilocker) is a key initiative under Digital India, the Government of India's flagship program.

questions.question: Digital Locker is a platform for insurance and verification of documents and certificates in:

options.B: A digital way (eliminating use of physical documents)

questions.explanation: Page 496: Digital Locker is a platform for insurance and verification of documents and certificates in a digital way, eliminating use of physical documents.

questions.question: Indian residents who signup for a DigiLocker account get a dedicated:
questions.explanation: Page 496: Indian residents who signup get a dedicated cloud storage space.

questions.question: Over how many crore users are registered with DigiLocker platform?
questions.explanation: Page 496: Over 29.61 crore users are registered with this platform.

questions.question: Over how many crore documents are accessible via Digi Locker?
questions.explanation: Page 496: Over 675 crore documents are accessible via Digi Locker.

questions.question: PRAGATI stands for:

options.C: Public Resource And Government Technology Initiative
options.D: Program for Active Government And Technology

questions.explanation: Page 496: PRAGATI: Pro-Active Governance And Timely Implementation.

questions.question: PRAGATI is a video conferencing facility part of Digital India programme for e-Governance, launched in:
questions.explanation: Page 496: PRAGATI video conferencing facility, part of Digital India programme, e-Governance Reforming Government through Technology, was launched in 2015.

questions.question: PRAGATI enables faster implementation of central level schemes, state level projects and resolutions of grievances between:
questions.explanation: Page 496: PRAGATI enables faster implementation of central level schemes/projects and resolutions of grievances between state and central level departments.

questions.question: MeitY has made e-Pramaan available for public usage with the help of:
questions.explanation: Page 496: MeitY has made e-Pramaan available for public usage with the help of C-DAC, Mumbai.

questions.question: Orthographic 3D Projection (a form of parallel projection) is a means of representing 3D objects in:
questions.explanation: Page 496: Orthographic 3D Projection (parallel projection) is a means of representing 3D objects in 2D.

questions.question: TAG Heuer has associated with which company to develop a smart luxury watch?
questions.explanation: Page 496: TAG Heuer, a premium watchmaker, has associated with Google to develop a smart luxury watch.

questions.question: NKN connects SDCs (State Data Centre), NDCs (National Data Centre), Ministries and departments across:
questions.explanation: Page 496: NKN connects all SDCs, NDCs, Ministries and departments across the government and educational institutions.

questions.question: India's first payment ecosystem for transport based on NCMC standards was launched on:
questions.explanation: Page 496: India's first payment ecosystem for transport based on NCMC was launched on 4th March, 2019.

questions.question: cVIGIL is an innovative mobile application for citizens to report:

options.B: Model Code of Conduct and Expenditure violations during elections

questions.explanation: Page 496: cVIGIL is for citizens to report Model Code of Conduct and Expenditure violations during elections.

questions.question: An IC can hold anywhere from hundreds to:

options.B: Millions of transistors, resistors and capacitors

questions.explanation: Page 496: An IC can hold anywhere from hundreds to millions of transistors, resistors and capacitors.

questions.question: e-Sign can be integrated with service delivery applications via:
questions.explanation: Page 496: e-Sign is an online electronic signature service which can be integrated with service delivery applications via an API.

questions.question: Digital India's flagship program is aimed at transforming India into a digitally empowered society and:
questions.explanation: Page 496: Digital India's flagship program aimed at transforming India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy.
